result.stdout:
Description:
  JSON .NET Global Tool

Usage:
  dotnet-json [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  merge <file> <files>      merge two or more json files into one
  set <file> <key> <value>  set a value in a json file
  remove, rm <file> <key>   Remove a value from the json
  get <file> <key>          Read a value from a JSON file.
  indent <file>             read a json file and write it out with correct indentation

commands.payload:
Description:
  JSON .NET Global Tool

Usage:
  dotnet-json [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  merge <file> <files>      merge two or more json files into one
  set <file> <key> <value>  set a value in a json file
  remove, rm <file> <key>   Remove a value from the json
  get <file> <key>          Read a value from a JSON file.
  indent <file>             read a json file and write it out with correct indentation

result.stdout:
Description:
  Read a value from a JSON file.

Usage:
  dotnet-json get <file> <key> [options]

Arguments:
  <file>  The JSON file (use '-' for STDIN)
  <key>   The key to get (use ':' to get a nested object and use index numbers to get array values eg. nested:key or nested:1:key)

Options:
  -e, --exact     only return exact value matches, this will return an error for references to nested objects/arrays.
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Read a value from a JSON file.

Usage:
  dotnet-json get <file> <key> [options]

Arguments:
  <file>  The JSON file (use '-' for STDIN)
  <key>   The key to get (use ':' to get a nested object and use index numbers to get array values eg. nested:key or nested:1:key)

Options:
  -e, --exact     only return exact value matches, this will return an error for references to nested objects/arrays.
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  read a json file and write it out with correct indentation

Usage:
  dotnet-json indent <file> [options]

Arguments:
  <file>  The JSON file (use '-' for STDIN)

Options:
  -o, --output <output>  The output file (use '-' for STDOUT, defaults to <file>)
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  read a json file and write it out with correct indentation

Usage:
  dotnet-json indent <file> [options]

Arguments:

Options:

  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  merge two or more json files into one

Usage:
  dotnet-json merge <file> <files>... [options]

Arguments:
  <file>   The JSON file (use '-' for STDIN)
  <files>  The names of the files to merge with the first file.

Options:
  -o, --output <output>  The output file (use '-' for STDOUT, defaults to <file>)
  -c, --compressed       Write the output in compressed form (defaults to indented)
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  merge two or more json files into one

Usage:
  dotnet-json merge <file> <files>... [options]

Arguments:
  <file>   The JSON file (use '-' for STDIN)
  <files>  The names of the files to merge with the first file.

Options:
  -o, --output <output>  The output file (use '-' for STDOUT, defaults to <file>)
  -c, --compressed       Write the output in compressed form (defaults to indented)
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Remove a value from the json

Usage:
  dotnet-json remove <file> <key> [options]

Arguments:
  <file>  The JSON file (use '-' for STDIN)
  <key>   The JSON key to remove

Options:
  -o, --output <output>  The output file (use '-' for STDOUT, defaults to <file>)
  -c, --compressed       Write the output in compressed form (defaults to indented)
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Remove a value from the json

Usage:
  dotnet-json remove <file> <key> [options]

Arguments:
  <file>  The JSON file (use '-' for STDIN)
  <key>   The JSON key to remove

Options:
  -o, --output <output>  The output file (use '-' for STDOUT, defaults to <file>)
  -c, --compressed       Write the output in compressed form (defaults to indented)
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  set a value in a json file

Usage:
  dotnet-json set <file> <key> <value> [options]

Arguments:
  <file>   The JSON file (use '-' for STDIN)
  <key>    The key to set (use ':' to set nested object and use index numbers to set array values eg. nested:key or nested:1:key)
  <value>  The value to set

Options:
  -o, --output <output>  The output file (use '-' for STDOUT, defaults to <file>)
  -e, --existing         Only set the value if the key already exists in the json file, otherwise do nothing
  -c, --compressed       Write the output in compressed form (defaults to indented)
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  set a value in a json file

Usage:
  dotnet-json set <file> <key> <value> [options]

Arguments:
  <file>   The JSON file (use '-' for STDIN)
  <key>    The key to set (use ':' to set nested object and use index numbers to set array values eg. nested:key or nested:1:key)
  <value>  The value to set

Options:
  -o, --output <output>  The output file (use '-' for STDOUT, defaults to <file>)
  -e, --existing         Only set the value if the key already exists in the json file, otherwise do nothing
  -c, --compressed       Write the output in compressed form (defaults to indented)
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information
